talk_metadata.talk_title: Measuring cancer evolution with mathematical modeling
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we may even undersample or oversample, mostly undersample selection events"
Speaker said: "we have to be aware of that" when discussing spatial sampling bias

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Assumes a well‑mixed tumor population; fails to capture spatial subclonal structure

key_data_gaps: No single‑cell or spatial validation of subclonal frequency predictions
No clinical outcome data linking model parameters to patient survival

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for spatially resolved biomarkers to validate subclonal frequency estimates and improve model calibration.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we need spatial data before modeling crazy scenarios that we can't possibly measure"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate spatial omics with evolutionary modeling to generate robust subclonal biomarkers.

unresolved_questions: How to accurately infer selection coefficients from bulk VAF data in spatially heterogeneous tumors?
What is the minimal sampling density required to avoid undersampling of subclonal drivers?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Mathematical modeling drives adaptive therapy clinical trials
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Resistance should have a cost when compared to the transformed tumor cells that are not resistant"
Speaker said: "However, the 1205LU population appears to show no difference in proliferative ability"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Assumes resistant clones have no fitness cost; some resistant lines grow as fast as sensitive ones
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "the 1205LU population appears to show no difference in proliferative ability"
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted that a subset of patients responded dramatically to continuous therapy but later relapsed

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Adaptive therapy prostate cancer trial (abiraterone + Lupron)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Androgen receptor signaling in castration‑resistant prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker stratification for intrinsic resistance; all patients received same adaptive protocol
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline PSA dynamics or molecular markers to predict cost of resistance
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: Lack of early on‑treatment PSA kinetics to adjust adaptive thresholds
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small cohort (≈100 patients) limits power to detect subgroup effects

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on simple PSA‑based rule‑of‑thumb; may be circumvented by more sophisticated biomarker‑driven schedules
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad application without patient‑specific biomarker selection could dilute therapeutic signal

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that resistant cells often have a growth cost (e.g., MCF7 doxorubicin‑resistant cells are outcompeted) but then presented data where a resistant melanoma line (1205LU) showed no proliferative disadvantage, contradicting the general claim of a cost to resistance

key_data_gaps: Limited molecular profiling of resistant clones

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop computational biomarkers (e.g., PSA dynamics, tumor growth models) to pre‑select patients who will benefit from adaptive therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we could have done even better if we designed this trial where we had just created three arms to classify the patients based on their dynamics"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model individual PSA trajectories and predict optimal adaptive thresholds for each patient.

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate p38 inhibition (identified as a resistance mechanism) with adaptive dosing to enhance efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed p38 as a stress response driving resistance in prostate cancer models.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate multi‑drug adaptive schedules incorporating targeted agents like p38 inhibitors.

cited_competitors.name: Abiraterone
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care drug used continuously in the trial

cited_competitors.name: Lupron
cited_competitors.context: Combined with abiraterone in the adaptive protocol

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal PSA reduction threshold (25%, 50%, 75%) for switching on/off therapy?
Can early on‑treatment dynamics reliably predict long‑term adaptive therapy success?
